---
title: "Spatial Epidemiology and Applied Data Science"
subtitle: "DMgtAsia · Second Module (Intermediate level)"
author:
  - name: "Prof. (Dr.) Biju Soman"
    affiliation: "AMCHSS, SCTIMST, Trivandrum"
  - name: "Dr. Arun Mitra"
    affiliation: "AIIMS Bibinagar"
date: last-modified
---

![](dmgt_asia_logo.png){width=160 fig-align="left"}

## Welcome

This is the **Second Module (Intermediate level)** of the **DMgtAsia** course. It introduces
**spatial epidemiology and applied spatial data science** using R and the tidyverse,
progressing from foundational GIS concepts to hands-on disease-cluster detection on real
Indian public health datasets.

The material distils experience from **multiple spatial data science trainings and workshops
delivered across India**, consolidated and adapted for the DMgtAsia training framework.

## What you will be able to do

- Explain core GIS concepts — CRS/projections, spatial dependence, spatial autocorrelation,
  Tobler's First Law — and why they matter for public health.
- Perform spatial exploratory data analysis and apply CDC cartographic guidelines to design
  defensible public-health maps.
- Read, transform and join spatial data with `sf`; extract raster values; geocode addresses;
  and build publication-quality choropleths.
- Conduct and interpret global and local spatial autocorrelation (Moran's I, LISA) and map
  SIR/SMR to identify and communicate disease clustering.

## How the module is organised

The module is a four-unit arc. Each unit pairs a short lecture with hands-on R exercises and
a knowledge-check quiz.

| Session | Theme | Lectures | Hands-on |
|------|-------|----------|----------|
| **I** | Foundations of spatial thinking | Intro to GIS · Concepts in SDS | Reflection |
| **II** | Exploratory Spatial Data Analysis (ESDA) | Exploring data with R · Visualization principles | EDA on WHO TB data |
| **III** | Introduction to `sf` | The `sf` package | Exercises 1–4 |
| **IV** | Spatial Data Analysis & Clustering | (lab-led) | Exercises 6–7 · John Snow · SIR/SMR · Capstone |

See the full session list and run order in the navigation bar, or jump to **[Setup](setup.qmd)**
to prepare your machine first.

## Delivery

- **Self-paced:** 4 weeks, one unit per week.
- **Intensive workshop:** 2 days — Day 1 Units A–B, Day 2 Units C–D.

## Acknowledgements

Developed at the **Achutha Menon Centre for Health Science Studies, SCTIMST** and
**AIIMS Bibinagar**. Built on the open `sf`, `spdep`, `rgeoda`, `ggspatial` and `tidyverse`
ecosystems. Data: NMC, NFHS, WHO, SEDAC GPWv4, and `HistData`.
